repo.name: scrapy/scrapy
repo.scope: the Scrapy web-crawling framework (Python, Twisted async)

reference_claims.text: There is a Scrapy execution Engine component.
There is a Scheduler component.
There is a Downloader component.
There are Spiders: custom classes (a Spider base class) written by users.
There is an Item Pipeline component.
There are Downloader middlewares (a downloader-middleware layer/manager).
There are Spider middlewares (a spider-middleware layer/manager).
The Engine is responsible for controlling the data flow between all components of the system and triggering events when certain actions occur.
The Scheduler receives requests from the engine and enqueues them for feeding them later (back to the engine) when the engine requests them.
The Downloader is responsible for fetching web pages and feeding them to the engine (which feeds them to the spiders).
Spiders parse responses and extract items from them or additional requests to follow.
The Item Pipeline processes items once they have been extracted by the spiders (typical tasks: cleansing, validation, persistence).
Downloader middlewares are hooks that sit between the Engine and the Downloader and process requests (Engine->Downloader) and responses (Downloader->Engine).
Spider middlewares are hooks that sit between the Engine and the Spiders and process spider input (responses) and output (items and requests).
The Engine schedules requests in the Scheduler and asks it for the next requests to crawl (Engine<->Scheduler).
The Engine sends requests to the Downloader and receives the generated Responses (Engine<->Downloader).
The Engine sends Responses to the Spider for processing and receives scraped items and new requests (Engine<->Spider).
The Engine sends processed (scraped) items to the Item Pipelines.
All requests and responses passing between the Engine and the Downloader pass through the Downloader middlewares.
All spider input (responses) and output (items/requests) passing between the Engine and the Spiders pass through the Spider middlewares.
The data flow is controlled centrally by the execution engine; the engine mediates between the other components rather than them being wired directly to each other.
The crawl loop repeats: the engine keeps asking the scheduler for the next requests until there are no more requests from the scheduler.
The crawl begins with the Engine getting the initial Requests to crawl from the Spider (start requests).
Scrapy is written with Twisted, an event-driven networking framework, and is implemented using non-blocking (asynchronous) code for concurrency.

emitted_claims.text: cmdline (CLI entry point) ג€” sole user-facing executable + command dispatcher.
commands ג€” one `class Command` per `scrapy <command>` operation.
crawler (lifecycle orchestration) ג€” owns the full crawl lifecycle, reactor/loop, settings, signals.
core.engine (ExecutionEngine) ג€” central control loop coordinating scheduler/downloader/scraper.
core.scheduler ג€” stores, prioritizes and dedups requests for the engine.
core.downloader ג€” fetches a Request via middleware chain + per-key concurrency Slot.
core.downloader.handlers ג€” pluggable per-URL-scheme transport layer.
core.http2 ג€” opt-in HTTP/2 client for the downloader (on the `h2` library).
core.scraper ג€” parses responses, routes through spider middleware + item pipelines.
middleware (MiddlewareManager base) ג€” abstract base for all middleware managers.
core.spidermw (SpiderMiddlewareManager) ג€” concrete spider-side middleware manager.
spidermiddlewares ג€” built-in spider middlewares (depth/httperror/referer/urllength/start).
downloadermiddlewares ג€” built-in downloader middlewares (cookies/retry/redirect/cache/...).
pipelines (ItemPipelineManager + media/files/images) ג€” item-pipeline subsystem + media download family.
spiders ג€” user-extension surface: base Spider + built-in spider types.
extensions ג€” optional signal-wired runtime add-ons.
http (Request/Response model) ג€” canonical in-memory data model for one HTTP exchange.
selector ג€” thin parsel subclass layer for XPath/CSS/JSON extraction.
linkextractors ג€” synchronous follow-worthy-link extraction library.
settings ג€” priority-based dict-like configuration system.
loader ג€” ItemLoader subclass of external itemloaders.
exporters ג€” item serialization family (JSON/XML/CSV/Pickle/...).
contracts ג€” spider-contracts testing subsystem.
extras ג€” non-importable distribution assets (completions/man/bench).
utils (shared library) ג€” cross-cutting helpers + Twisted/asyncio bridge.
cmdline -> commands: imports command base classes and reflectively discovers/instantiates command classes under `scrapy.commands`.
cmdline -> crawler: imports Async/CrawlerProcess and constructs one into `cmd.crawler_process`.
cmdline -> settings: imports BaseSettings/Settings and builds process settings via `get_project_settings` plus command setdict defaults.
cmdline -> utils: imports walk_modules_iter, get_project_settings/inside_project, garbage_collect, _asyncio_reactor_path.
commands -> crawler: runtime-call `crawler_process.crawl/.start`.
commands -> core.engine: runtime-call `engine.start_async` / `pcrawler.engine.scraper.itemproc`.
commands -> contracts: imports `ContractsManager`.
crawler -> core.engine: imports and constructs `ExecutionEngine`.
crawler -> spiders: imports Spider and creates it via `spidercls.from_crawler`.
crawler -> extensions: builds `self.extensions` via `ExtensionManager.from_crawler`.
core.engine -> core.scheduler: imports BaseScheduler, issubclass-checks the configured class, and calls `next_request()`.
core.engine -> core.scraper: imports Scraper, constructs `Scraper(crawler)`, drives `enqueue_scrape`.
core.engine -> core.downloader: settings-resolved `load_object(DOWNLOADER)` then constructs and calls `fetch`.
core.scheduler -> dupefilters / pqueues / statscollectors / http / spiders / utils, all resolved via settings load_object.
core.downloader -> core.downloader.handlers: owns a `DownloadHandlers` instance and dispatches transfer to it.
core.downloader -> downloadermiddlewares: builds the DOWNLOADER_MIDDLEWARES chain.
core.downloader -> middleware: DownloaderMiddlewareManager subclasses the shared MiddlewareManager base.
core.downloader.handlers -> core.http2: `H2DownloadHandler` imports H2Agent/H2ConnectionPool.
core.http2 -> core.downloader: imports `_AcceptableProtocolsContextFactory`.
core.scraper -> core.spidermw / pipelines / core.engine / http / spiders.
core.spidermw -> middleware (subclass) + spidermiddlewares (config-load SPIDER_MIDDLEWARES_BASE).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Technical dependencies (spidermiddlewares invoked inbound by core.spidermw)
emitted_claims.text: spidermiddlewares are invoked INBOUND by core.spidermw (they depend on http/spiders/utils, never on the manager).
downloadermiddlewares -> core.engine: RobotsTxt calls `crawler.engine.download_async`.
pipelines -> core.engine: media pipeline calls `engine.download_async` (media egress goes through the engine).
pipelines -> middleware: ItemPipelineManager subclasses the MiddlewareManager base.
spiders -> http / linkextractors / selector / utils / crawler(DI).
extensions -> exporters: feedexport loads FEED_EXPORTERS BaseItemExporter subclasses; ExtensionManager loads extensions inversely.
http -> selector: deferred import to break a cycle (`Selector(self)` inside TextResponse.selector).
selector -> http / utils / external parsel.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Technical dependencies (exporters consumed inbound by feedexport)
emitted_claims.text: exporters -> utils/item/itemadapter; consumed INBOUND by feedexport.
contracts -> http / utils / settings (registration-by-consumer, SPIDER_CONTRACTS_BASE).
commands -> spiderloader: `get_spider_loader`; DummySpiderLoader used by runspider.
crawler -> addons: imports AddonManager, loads settings and per-crawler add-on instances.
crawler -> signals: imports SignalManager as `self.signals`.
core.engine -> scrapy.signals: fires engine_started/stopped, spider_opened and other lifecycle signals via the SignalManager.
extensions -> core.downloader: AutoThrottle mutates `engine.downloader.slots[].delay` and type-imports Slot.
extras depends INTO the framework (http/spiders/commands/cmdline) one-way; nothing in scrapy/ imports from extras.
Scrapy owns NO central database; almost all component state is per-process in-memory, with durable persistence only at optional pluggable edges.
Scheduler job directory `<JOBDIR>/requests.queue/` is created when JOBDIR is set (crawl resume).
`active.json` is a JSON snapshot of priority-queue state (startprios), read on open/resume and written on clean close.
HTTP cache persistence is pluggable via HTTPCACHE_STORAGE (DbmCacheStorage dbm file, FilesystemCacheStorage dir per request) keyed by request fingerprint.
Feed export uses pluggable IFeedStorage backends (local file / stdout / S3-boto3 / GCS / FTP) selected by scheme from FEED_STORAGES.
Media/files/images storage writes via STORE_SCHEMES registry (file->FSFilesStore, s3->S3FilesStore, gs->GCSFilesStore, ftp->FTPFilesStore) reached only through the FilesStoreProtocol interface.
Media storage is content-addressed by sha1(request.url) -> full/{sha1}{ext}; images full/{sha1}.jpg, thumbs thumbs/{thumb_id}/{sha1}.jpg; checksums are md5 of the body.
SpiderState pickles `spider.state` to `<jobdir>/spider.state` on close and reloads it on open (pickle protocol 4).
Stats are a shared read/write StatsCollector (not owned by any one component): corestats/logstats/middlewares/scheduler all increment the same crawler StatsCollector.
`request.meta` is the documented cross-middleware shared dict (retry_times, redirect_times/urls, proxy, cached_response, download_slot, dont_filter).

core.downloader holds the live `self.slots: dict[str, Slot]` table (GC'd every 60s) and `self.active: set[Request]`, reading module-global `dnscache = LocalCache(10000)` from scrapy.resolver for IP-concurrency slot keys.
http CookieJar is an in-memory adapter over stdlib http.cookiejar.CookieJar; cookie store is nested dict domain->path->name->Cookie.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Critical to know / critical-facts (system boot chain)
emitted_claims.text: User entry is the `scrapy` console command declared `scrapy = scrapy.cmdline:execute`; `execute()` resolves settings, discovers commands, parses argv, constructs a CrawlerProcess if required, runs the command, then `sys.exit(cmd.exitcode)`.
The Process layer drives `process.crawl` (-> Crawler.crawl) then `process.start()`, which sets up and runs the reactor/loop and BLOCKS until the crawl finishes.

`Crawler.crawl_async` creates the spider, applies+freezes settings, then `self.engine = ExecutionEngine(...)` -> `open_spider_async()` -> `start_async()`, which blocks on `self._closewait` until the crawl finishes.

Mediator boundary: Scheduler/Downloader/Scraper never reach across each other ג€” the engine pulls from `scheduler.next_request()`, pushes to `downloader.fetch()`, routes output to `scraper.enqueue_scrape()`.

Inversion of control: spiders never import/call the engine or scraper; the core calls into the spider (scraper uses `spider._parse` default callback, spidermw consumes `spider.start()` as crawl seed).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Boundary rules (layering down-only at leaves)
emitted_claims.text: Layering DOWN-only at the leaves: http, selector, linkextractors, exporters, settings, utils import no orchestration component ג€” flow code depends on the model, not vice versa.
Engine -> Downloader is one-directional: core.downloader never imports core.engine, only the injected Crawler (TYPE_CHECKING-only).

Reactor-isolation rule (project-wide, lint-enforced): module-level imports of twisted.internet.reactor (and twisted.protocols.ftp) are banned; the reactor is imported lazily inside the method that needs it.

Error-propagation convention: log/return and continue, do not crash the loop after startup (engine errbacks only log.info; scheduler rejection returned as a bool; middlewares filter by raising IgnoreRequest; CLI errors surface as sys.exit).

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Boundary rules (security boundary on redirect)
emitted_claims.text: Security boundary on redirect: Cookie stripped on cross-host/scheme-downgrade and Authorization stripped on any scheme/host/port change per WHATWG fetch.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Boundary rules (module isolation: required chain)
emitted_claims.text: Module isolation: a request must pass the downloader-middleware chain before any transfer (`fetch` wraps `_enqueue_request` inside `middleware.download_async`); spider-side the callback is wrapped with input/output/exception hooks.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Boundary rules (extensions are observers, feedexport exception)
emitted_claims.text: Extensions are a lifecycle-observer layer, never in the Request->Response chain ג€” they observe via signals; the structural exception is feed export serializing items at `item_scraped`.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Boundary rules (type guards at engine boundary)
emitted_claims.text: Type guards at the engine boundary: downloader output must be Request/Response/Failure and fetch result Response/Request else TypeError; middleware output contract enforced or `_InvalidOutput`.
Layered exit-status ownership: the process exits with `cmd.exitcode` (default 0); the command, not cmdline, decides status; crawl/runspider set exitcode=1 when bootstrap_failed.
HttpAuth raises ValueError if HTTPAUTH_USER/PASS is set without an explicit HTTPAUTH_DOMAIN, preventing credential leakage to all hosts.
HTTP/2 hostname-isolation: a stream verifies request host == connected peer before sending.

ORDERED OPEN (engine open_spider_async): set spider -> CallLaterOnce(_start_scheduled_requests) -> build scheduler -> create _Slot -> spidermw.process_start() -> optional scheduler.open() -> scraper.open_spider_async() -> stats.open_spider() -> send spider_opened.

ORDERED CLOSE (engine close_spider_async): slot.close() -> downloader.close() -> scraper.close_spider_async() -> scheduler.close(reason) -> send spider_closed -> stats.close_spider() -> null _slot+spider -> _spider_closed_callback; each step independently try/except-logged.
Single-slot invariant: only one spider open at a time ג€” opening while `self._slot is not None` raises 'No free spider slot'.
Drain invariant: `_Slot.close()` does not fire until `inprogress` is empty, so in-flight requests drain before close.
Single-use invariant: a Crawler may run once; `crawl()/crawl_async()` raise RuntimeError if already crawling/started.
Server/loop extensions start on engine_started and tear down on engine_stopped; pipelines tear down in reverse (LIFO) via `appendleft(close_spider)`.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Lifecycle (pipelines open/close parallel, process_item sequential)
emitted_claims.text: Pipelines open/close run all pipelines in PARALLEL while `process_item` runs them SEQUENTIALLY.

Scheduler ordered lifecycle: from_crawler -> open(spider) builds mqs, conditionally dqs (resuming from active.json, logs 'Resuming crawl') -> steady state -> close(reason) dumps disk-queue state to active.json.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Lifecycle (spider must have a name)
emitted_claims.text: A spider must have a name (`__init__` raises ValueError otherwise); `_set_crawler` connects `self.close` to `spider_closed`.

Request/Response value-semantics via copy-on-replace: `copy()`/`replace(**kw)` build a NEW instance from the `attributes` tuple, never mutating in place; the `attributes` tuple is the single source of truth for round-trip fields.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Key invariants (__slots__ in http model)
emitted_claims.text: `__slots__` everywhere in the http model; mutable attrs (meta/headers/cookies/flags) lazy-init a fresh dict/list only on first access.
Settings immutable-snapshot idiom: `frozencopy()` = `copy()` (full deepcopy) then `freeze()`; `getlist`/`getdict` return NEW copies.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Key invariants (downloader Slot data structure)
emitted_claims.text: Downloader core data structure: `Slot = @dataclass(slots=True, eq=False)` with active/transferring sets + FIFO `deque[(Request, Deferred)]` + a single `latercall` mutex blocking `_process_queue` until the delay timer fires.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Key invariants (downloader ordering 'Do not change!')
emitted_claims.text: Downloader load-bearing ORDERING invariant marked 'Do not change!': `_download` awaits transfer, fires response_downloaded BEFORE pulling the next queued request, then removes from transferring + re-pumps + fires request_left_downloader in finally.

MiddlewareManager core data structure: `self.methods: dict[str, deque[Callable|None]]`, one ordered deque per hook; subclasses `append` request-side and `appendleft` response/exception-side so the response chain runs in reverse of the request chain.
Scheduler two-tier queue: single memory PQ by default; a disk PQ added only when JOBDIR is set; enqueue is disk-first/memory-fallback, dequeue is memory-first/disk-fallback.
Dedup gate ordering invariant: `dupefilter.request_seen` is checked BEFORE any enqueue ג€” a seen request (unless dont_filter) is dropped and never queued.
Settings priority layering: default(0) < command(10) < addon(15) < project(20) < spider(30) < cmdline(40); a set takes effect only if priority >= stored.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Key invariants (Headers multi-valued caseless dict)
emitted_claims.text: Headers is a multi-valued caseless dict: keys title-cased to bytes, values stored as list[bytes]; __getitem__/get return the LAST value, getlist returns all; empty value-list yields None not KeyError.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Key invariants (scraper accounting symmetry / backpressure)
emitted_claims.text: Scraper accounting symmetry: active_size incremented in add_response_request and decremented by the identical max(len(body),1024) in finish_response; backpressure once active_size > max_active_size (default 5,000,000).

HTTP/2 StreamCloseReason is an 8-value Enum, the single dispatch key for stream termination; body built in a BytesIO and converted exactly once via make_response when the stream ENDs; client streams use odd ids.
Request fingerprint is SHA1 over canonicalized url+method+body; headers ignored by default; memoized in a per-Request WeakKeyDictionary.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Key invariants (media dedup, scheduler LIFO/DFO)
emitted_claims.text: Media dedup: each media request keyed by request fingerprint, a URL downloaded at most once per spider run; default scheduler crawl order is LIFO -> DFO.
Twisted (>=21.7.0) is the core async substrate (Deferred/inlineCallbacks throughout), bridged to native asyncio ג€” AsyncioSelectorReactor is the default reactor.
Components are pluggable via settings + `load_object`/`build_from_crawler`: DOWNLOADER, SCHEDULER, DUPEFILTER_CLASS, ITEM_PROCESSOR, DOWNLOAD_HANDLERS, etc.
lxml + parsel + cssselect + w3lib for parsing/selectors; queuelib backs the scheduler queues.

emitted_claims.source_region: AGENTS.md Technology choices (HTTP/2 via optional h2)
emitted_claims.text: HTTP/2 via the optional `h2` (hyper-h2) library, NOT in the hard dependencies, loaded only when an HTTP/2 download is requested.
Python >=3.10, single hatchling-built distribution Scrapy v2.16.0 with tool.hatch.version reading scrapy/VERSION.
zope.interface for component interfaces (IFeedStorage(Interface)).
PyDispatcher (pydispatch) for the signals/event bus; queuelib backs the disk/memory request queues.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Technology choices (TLS stack, non-verifying default)
emitted_claims.text: cryptography + pyOpenSSL + service_identity for TLS; TLS default is non-verifying ג€” cert errors logged/ignored unless DOWNLOAD_VERIFY_CERTIFICATES is set.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Technology choices (robots.txt, item abstraction, tldextract)
emitted_claims.text: protego + robotexclusionrulesparser for robots.txt; itemadapter + itemloaders for item abstraction; tldextract for domain handling.
In-flight async migration: across engine/scraper/spidermw/pipelines every sync public method is a deprecated Deferred-returning shim over an `_async` coroutine; the `_async` methods are the real API.

Scheduler default wiring: SCHEDULER=Scheduler, DUPEFILTER_CLASS=RFPDupeFilter, SCHEDULER_PRIORITY_QUEUE=DownloaderAwarePriorityQueue, memory LIFO / disk Pickle-LIFO queues, FIFO start queues, JOBDIR=None.

emitted_claims.source_region: core.scheduler.md Boundary rules (duck-typed interface via metaclass)
emitted_claims.text: BaseScheduler interface conformance is duck-typed via a metaclass (__subclasscheck__ accepts any class exposing has_pending_requests/enqueue_request/next_request), enforced by the engine's issubclass check before construction.
enqueue_request returns a bool, never raises, to signal rejection: False means dropped (dupefilter rejection), prompting the engine's request_dropped signal.

emitted_claims.source_region: core.downloader.md Boundary rules (handler dispatch by scheme)
emitted_claims.text: Handler dispatch is by URL scheme only and refuses unknown schemes (NotSupported) rather than crashing the run.
Downloader concurrency gate: a slot transfers only while free_transfer_slots() = concurrency - len(transferring) > 0; global backout triggers when len(self.active) >= total_concurrency.
Scraper reads ITEM_PROCESSOR (default ItemPipelineManager), CONCURRENT_ITEMS (default 100), SCRAPER_SLOT_MAX_ACTIVE_SIZE (default 5000000).
str-vs-bytes body invariant: a base Response body MUST be bytes (raises TypeError directing to TextResponse/HtmlResponse for unicode); TextResponse is the only place a str body is accepted.
text-only capability gate: the base Response raises NotSupported for css/xpath/jmespath; only TextResponse subtypes implement them.
Settings immutability gate: every mutator routes through _assert_mutability(), raising TypeError once frozen, and the Crawler freezes settings during _apply_settings.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Notable non-obvious facts (one file = one command, union of three sources)
emitted_claims.text: One file = one command: _iter_command_classes selects classes where obj.__module__ == module.__name__ and the CLI name is the module filename; the command set is the UNION of three sources (built-in scrapy.commands, the entry-point group, then COMMANDS_MODULE) layered with .update().

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Notable non-obvious facts (shell bypasses crawl-then-start)
emitted_claims.text: `shell` is the deliberate exception to the crawl-then-start chain ג€” it bypasses crawler_process.crawl() and manually creates the crawler + engine because the Shell hand-drives the engine.

emitted_claims.source_region: critical-facts.md Notable non-obvious facts (downloadermiddlewares/spidermiddlewares register nothing)
emitted_claims.text: The downloadermiddlewares/spidermiddlewares packages register nothing in __init__.py (both 0 bytes) ג€” wired in by dotted path in *_BASE settings; fixed spider-mw ordering Start(25)->HttpError(50)->Referer(700)->UrlLength(800)->Depth(900).

adjudication.referent: The engine crawl loop in `_start_scheduled_requests` (scrapy/core/engine.py:330-339) ג€” the `while not needs_backout(): if not _start_scheduled_request(): break` loop that repeatedly calls `scheduler.next_request()` (engine.py:364) and stops when it returns None (scheduler_empty).
adjudication.evidence:
RC-022's fact is the REPEAT/loop-until-empty invariant: the engine keeps asking the scheduler for the next request over and over until there are no more requests (the `while not self.needs_backout(): if not self._start_scheduled_request(): break` at engine.py:334-336, breaking when next_request() returns None / scheduler_empty fires at engine.py:364-367).

beyond_doc.referent: crawler (lifecycle orchestration) ג€” owns the full crawl lifecycle, reactor/loop, settings, signals
beyond_doc.note: Verified: class Crawler at scrapy/crawler.py. The doc's 7-component model has no Crawler/CrawlerProcess lifecycle owner above the Engine; the doc only describes the engine-mediated dataflow components, not the orchestration shell that constructs the engine and drives the reactor.

beyond_doc.referent: settings ג€” priority-based dict-like configuration system (BaseSettings)
beyond_doc.note: Verified: class BaseSettings(MutableMapping) at scrapy/settings/__init__.py:79; SETTINGS_PRIORITIES default(0)<command(10)<addon(15)<project(20)<spider(30)<cmdline(40). No RC mentions a settings/configuration component at all.

beyond_doc.referent: Scrapy owns NO central database; component state is per-process in-memory with durable persistence only at optional pluggable edges
beyond_doc.note: Verified against scheduler/cache/feed/media storage code. The doc has zero data-store claims; this whole data-store kind (EC-063..074: JOBDIR queue, active.json, HTTP cache, feed/media stores, request.meta, stats, cookiejar) is entirely beyond-doc.

beyond_doc.referent: User entry is the `scrapy` console command `scrapy = scrapy.cmdline:execute`
beyond_doc.note: Verified: pyproject.toml:64 declares scrapy = "scrapy.cmdline:execute"; cmdline.py:169 def execute(). RC-023 covers a different entry concept (engine getting start requests from the spider), not the CLI/process bootstrap layer.

beyond_doc.referent: ORDERED OPEN sequence in engine.open_spider_async (set spider -> CallLaterOnce -> build scheduler -> create _Slot -> spidermw.process_start -> scheduler.open -> scraper.open_spider_async -> stats.open_spider -> send spider_opened)
beyond_doc.note: Verified in scrapy/core/engine.py. The doc's only invariant is the crawl-loop-repeats claim (RC-022); the precise lifecycle ordering invariants (open/close order, single-slot, drain, single-use) are all omitted.

beyond_doc.referent: lxml + parsel + cssselect + w3lib for parsing/selectors; queuelib backs the scheduler queues
beyond_doc.note: Verified in pyproject.toml dependencies (cssselect, lxml, parsel, queuelib, w3lib). RC-024 names only Twisted/async; every other technology choice (parsing libs, zope.interface, PyDispatcher, TLS stack, Python>=3.10/hatchling, optional h2) is beyond-doc.

beyond_doc.referent: Security boundary on redirect: Cookie stripped on cross-host/scheme-downgrade and Authorization stripped on any scheme/host/port change per WHATWG fetch
beyond_doc.note: Verified in scrapy/downloadermiddlewares/redirect.py. The doc's boundary rules cover only the middleware/mediator structure (RC-019/020/021); security/type/error-propagation boundary rules are all omitted.

beyond_doc.referent: core.engine -> core.scraper: engine imports Scraper, constructs Scraper(crawler), drives enqueue_scrape
beyond_doc.note: Verified in scrapy/core/engine.py. The doc models engine<->spider directly (RC-017); the internal Scraper component that routes responses through spider-middleware + pipelines is a finer-grained edge the doc never names.

scoring_provenance.method: isolated-agent scoring (separation of duties): a key-reader + an EC-extractor (BLIND to the key) + one matcher per RC + a faithfulness refuter per overlap (vs CODE) + a beyond-doc finder. Workflow  ( post-fix v2).
scoring_provenance.orchestrator_caveat: The orchestrating session authored the curator keys, so it is not a fully fresh scorer; but every EC-extraction, match, and faithfulness JUDGMENT was made by an isolated agent (the EC-extractor blind to the key). Recorded honestly.

caveats: INDICATIVE — small-n, fuzzy truth, below any ship-gate
attribution-adjudicated — divergences resolved to CODE, not auto-scored
doc-bounded — agreement with a good human doc, not absolute completeness
training-contamination — scrapy is a public repo; the blind output may be informed by pretraining, so the number is INDICATIVE.
